items.question: This box is far too heavy — I ___ lift it.
explanations: Correct! Inability takes 'can't': 'I can't lift it'.
'Mustn't lift' would mean lifting is forbidden.
'Don't have to lift' means it's optional.
Never add 'to' after 'can't'.

items.question: During the test, you ___ look at your neighbour's answers.
explanations: 'Can't' can report a rule, but 'mustn't' states the prohibition more firmly here.
Correct! A firm prohibition takes 'mustn't': 'You mustn't look at their answers'.
'Don't have to look' means it's optional.
Never add 'to' after 'mustn't'.

items.question: That ___ be Sara — she's in Australia right now.
explanations: 'Mustn't' is never used for a deduction.
Correct! A negative deduction takes 'can't': 'That can't be Sara'.
'Don't have to be' doesn't express impossibility.
'Doesn't must' is not correct English.

items.question: You ___ touch the paintings in the museum.
explanations: 'Can't touch' reports what's not allowed, but 'mustn't' states the rule more strongly.
Correct! A firm rule takes 'mustn't': 'You mustn't touch the paintings'.
'Don't have to touch' means it's optional.
'Aren't able' is about ability, not prohibition.

items.question: He ___ have finished the whole book already — he only started today!
explanations: Correct! Impossibility about the past takes 'can't have': 'He can't have finished'.
'Mustn't' is never used for deductions.
'Doesn't have to finish' means it's optional.
'Mustn't have' is not used for a guess.

items.question: I'm sorry, I ___ come to your party — I'll be away.
explanations: Correct! Being unable due to circumstances takes 'can't': 'I can't come'.
'Mustn't come' would mean you're forbidden to come.
'Don't have to come' means it's optional.
Never add 'to' after 'can't'.

items.question: We ___ forget to send the invoice, or we won't get paid.
explanations: 'Can't forget' would mean it's impossible to forget.
Correct! A strong 'don't' takes 'mustn't': 'We mustn't forget to send the invoice'.
'Don't have to forget' makes no sense here.
'Aren't able to forget' is about ability.

items.question: Correct this: 'That mustn't be the right answer.'
options: That can't be the right answer.
That mustn't to be the right answer.
That doesn't have to be the right answer.
explanations: Correct! A negative deduction takes 'can't': 'That can't be the right answer'.
Never add 'to' after 'mustn't', and 'mustn't' is wrong for a guess.
'Doesn't have to be' changes the meaning to 'it isn't required'.
'Mustn't' can't be used for a deduction.

items.question: Which sentence expresses a PROHIBITION?
explanations: This is a reaction meaning 'that's impossible/unbelievable'.
Correct! 'You mustn't feed the animals' forbids the action.
This is inability.
This is a negative deduction.

items.question: The door's locked from inside, so she ___ have gone out this way.
explanations: Correct! Impossibility takes 'can't have': 'she can't have gone out this way'.
'Mustn't' is never used for deductions.
'Doesn't have to go' means it's optional.
'Mustn't have' is not used for a guess.
